articles.title: Understanding 1031 Exchanges for Multifamily Investors
articles.summary: An in-depth guide to how 1031 exchanges work, with a focus on multifamily properties and common timelines, rules, and pitfalls.
articles.content: A 1031 exchange allows real estate investors to defer capital gains taxes by reinvesting sale proceeds into a like-kind property. For multifamily investors, this can be a powerful tool to scale a portfolio without triggering immediate tax liability. In this article, we cover eligibility requirements, identification and closing timelines, qualified intermediaries, and how to underwrite replacement assets. We also walk through a sample exchange from a small duplex into a 100+ unit syndication, highlighting what to watch for in offering documents and sponsor disclosures.
articles.author: David Chen, CPA

articles.title: Step-by-Step 1031 Exchange Timeline Checklist
articles.summary: A practical checklist covering the 45-day identification and 180-day closing requirements in a 1031 exchange.
articles.content: Timing is one of the most common failure points in a 1031 exchange. Investors have 45 days from the sale of their relinquished property to identify replacement assets, and 180 days to close. This article provides a chronological checklist, from engaging a qualified intermediary before closing, to drafting identification letters, coordinating with lenders and sponsors, and documenting any failed or partial exchanges. We include downloadable templates you can adapt for your own exchange plan.
articles.author: Laura Martinez, Esq.

articles.title: 1031 Exchange Risks and Pitfalls Every Investor Should Know
articles.summary: Key legal, tax, and execution risks that can cause a 1031 exchange to fail, and how to mitigate them.
articles.content: While 1031 exchanges offer powerful tax benefits, they also introduce additional complexity and risk. Common pitfalls include missing critical deadlines, selecting ineligible replacement properties, commingling funds, and misunderstanding boot. In this article we outline the most frequent issues we see investors encounter, how sponsors typically structure 1031-friendly syndications or DSTs, and the questions you should ask before wiring exchange proceeds into a new deal.
articles.author: Karen O'Neill, JD, LLM

articles.title: How to Evaluate a Multifamily Deal in 15 Minutes
articles.summary: A quick framework for screening multifamily offerings using five key metrics and qualitative checks.
articles.content: Busy investors need a repeatable way to screen multifamily offerings. In this guide, we walk through five key areas: sponsorship, location, business plan, leverage, and projected returns. We explain how to use metrics like DSCR, debt yield, cash-on-cash, and IRR to quickly categorize a deal as core, value-add, or opportunistic, and when a fast "no" is the right answer.
articles.author: Mark Rivera

articles.title: Office Investing in a Hybrid Work World
articles.summary: How hybrid work trends are reshaping office demand, lease structures, and value-add strategies.
articles.content: The shift to hybrid work has created a wide dispersion in office performance. Well-located, amenitized buildings continue to attract tenants, while commodity space struggles. We discuss how investors are underwriting future occupancy, TI packages, and capex in this new environment, and highlight case studies from our own office repositioning deals.
articles.author: Rachel Kim

articles.title: Industrial Warehouses: The Backbone of E-Commerce
articles.summary: Why last-mile and regional distribution facilities remain in high demand and how investors can participate.
articles.content: Industrial real estate has been one of the strongest-performing sectors over the past decade. This article explores demand drivers such as e-commerce penetration, supply chain restructuring, and onshoring. We break down common lease structures, tenant credit considerations, and key risks like obsolescence and oversupply in certain submarkets.
articles.author: James Patel

articles.title: Tax Strategies for Passive Real Estate Investors
articles.summary: An overview of depreciation, bonus depreciation, 1031 exchanges, and other tools for optimizing after-tax returns.
articles.content: Passive investors have access to powerful tax tools, from depreciation and cost segregation to 1031 exchanges and opportunity zones. We explain how K-1 losses can offset passive income, when to consider electing out of bonus depreciation, and how to coordinate with your CPA to avoid surprises. We also outline common misconceptions about "tax-free" cash flow.
articles.author: David Chen, CPA

articles.title: What Is IRR and Why It Matters in Real Estate Investing
articles.summary: A plain-English explanation of internal rate of return (IRR), with examples and common mistakes.
articles.content: Internal rate of return (IRR) is one of the most widely cited metrics in private real estate, but it is also frequently misunderstood. In this article we explain the math behind IRR, how the timing of cash flows affects it, and why a higher IRR does not always mean a better investment. We walk through sample cash flow projections and show how to compare deals with different hold periods.
articles.author: Emily Nguyen, CFA

articles.title: How Our Platform Sources and Underwrites Deals
articles.summary: A behind-the-scenes look at our acquisition pipeline, underwriting process, and investment committee.
articles.content: Investors often ask how we find and select deals. We walk through our sourcing channels, from broker relationships to off-market owners, and our multi-stage underwriting process. Each opportunity is reviewed by our acquisitions team, asset management group, and an independent investment committee before it is made available on the platform.
articles.author: Investment Committee

articles.title: Building a Diversified Commercial Real Estate Portfolio
articles.summary: How to combine multifamily, industrial, and office deals across markets to balance risk and return.
articles.content: Diversification in private real estate goes beyond owning more properties. We discuss how to think about asset class mix, geographic exposure, sponsor concentration, and leverage. We provide example model portfolios for conservative, moderate, and aggressive investors, and explain how our sample portfolio tool can help you test different allocations.
articles.author: Rachel Kim

articles.title: Beginner’s Guide to Commercial Real Estate Syndications
articles.summary: An introduction to how syndications are structured, how investors are paid, and what fees to expect.
articles.content: Syndications allow multiple investors to pool capital into larger commercial properties. This guide explains common structures (LP/GP, preferred equity), the typical flow of funds, when and how distributions are paid, and the fee types you might see. We also offer a checklist of questions to ask any sponsor before investing.
articles.author: Mark Rivera

articles.title: Using a Self-Directed IRA to Invest in Real Estate
articles.summary: How self-directed IRAs work, what rules govern them, and how to allocate to private real estate.
articles.content: Self-directed IRAs allow investors to hold alternative assets such as private real estate. We outline the account setup process, prohibited transactions, UBIT considerations, and how investments are titled in the name of the IRA custodian. We also discuss pros and cons of using retirement funds for illiquid investments.
articles.author: Karen O'Neill, JD, LLM

deals.title: South Congress Flats
deals.summary: Class B+ garden-style multifamily community in South Austin with value-add interior upgrades.
deals.full_description: South Congress Flats is a 212-unit, 1980s-vintage multifamily property located along Austin’s popular South Congress corridor. The business plan targets light interior renovations, common area enhancements, and operational efficiencies to drive rents while maintaining workforce affordability. The property benefits from proximity to major employers and strong population growth.

deals.title: East River Residences
deals.summary: Stabilized Class B community in East Austin targeting operational upside and modest renovations.
deals.full_description: East River Residences is a 168-unit property located minutes from downtown Austin and the growing East Riverside corridor. The sponsor plans to improve management, upgrade amenities, and selectively renovate units to capture rent premiums while preserving a strong in-place tenant base.

deals.title: Hill Country Lofts
deals.summary: Newer vintage Class A- loft-style community on the edge of Austin’s Hill Country.
deals.full_description: Hill Country Lofts is a 190-unit, 2015-built multifamily asset offering resort-style amenities and high-end finishes. The strategy focuses on strong in-place cash flow with modest rent growth and limited capital expenditures, targeting income-focused investors seeking exposure to Austin’s long-term growth story.

deals.title: Desert Vista Apartments
deals.summary: Well-located Phoenix multifamily community targeting interior renovations and operational upside.
deals.full_description: Desert Vista Apartments is a 176-unit Class B community in Phoenix’s rapidly growing West Valley. The sponsor plans a classic value-add strategy: upgrading interiors, enhancing curb appeal, and tightening operations to drive NOI and long-term appreciation.

deals.title: Bayfront Multifamily Fund
deals.summary: A diversified portfolio of three Class B+ assets across the Miami metro.
deals.full_description: Bayfront Multifamily Fund acquires and manages three stabilized, income-producing properties across Miami-Dade County. The strategy focuses on steady cash flow with selective value-add initiatives and disciplined leverage.

deals.title: Skyline Tower Offices
deals.summary: Downtown Denver Class A- office tower with a value-add leasing strategy.
deals.full_description: Skyline Tower is a 22-story office building located in Denver’s CBD. The sponsor plans to re-lease vacant floors, refresh common areas, and add flexible spec suites to attract smaller tenants seeking high-quality space in a convenient location.

deals.title: Harborview Office Center
deals.summary: Waterfront mid-rise office campus in Tampa with strong in-place tenancy and rollover upside.
deals.full_description: Harborview Office Center is a three-building, 310,000 SF office campus overlooking Tampa Bay. With a mix of credit tenants and upcoming lease expirations, the plan focuses on mark-to-market rent increases, targeted amenity upgrades, and sustainability improvements.

deals.title: Lakeshore Corporate Plaza
deals.summary: Institutional-quality office tower on Chicago’s lakefront.
deals.full_description: Lakeshore Corporate Plaza is a core-plus office opportunity with a stable rent roll and strong sponsorship. Modest leasing and amenity upgrades are expected to support continued occupancy and rent growth.

deals.title: Peachtree Business Plaza
deals.summary: Suburban Atlanta office park catering to medical and professional tenants.
deals.full_description: Peachtree Business Plaza consists of four low-rise office buildings in a high-traffic suburban corridor. The sponsor is targeting medical, legal, and professional services tenants with flexible suite sizes and competitive tenant improvement packages.

deals.title: Lone Star Logistics Hub
deals.summary: Modern cross-dock distribution facility serving the Dallas–Fort Worth metroplex.
deals.full_description: Lone Star Logistics Hub is a 410,000 SF, Class A distribution center with ample trailer parking and 32-foot clear heights. The strategy is to maintain high occupancy with credit tenants while capturing rent growth in one of the nation’s strongest industrial markets.

deals.title: Pacific Gateway Distribution Center
deals.summary: Institutional-quality logistics asset near the Port of Oakland.
deals.full_description: Pacific Gateway is a mission-critical distribution hub serving port-related tenants. With limited new supply and strong tenant demand, the property offers durable cash flow and long-term appreciation potential.

deals.title: Riverside Multifamily Fund I
deals.summary: Closed multifamily value-add portfolio in Sacramento with strong realized returns.
deals.full_description: Riverside Multifamily Fund I acquired and repositioned three Class B assets in Sacramento. Through targeted renovations and improved management, the sponsor exceeded original underwriting, delivering a 13.5% net IRR and 1.8x equity multiple over a 4.2-year hold.

deals.title: Midtown Office Tower I
deals.summary: Closed core-plus office investment in Charlotte’s Midtown submarket.
deals.full_description: Midtown Office Tower I executed a leasing and capital improvement strategy that increased occupancy from 78% to 93%, resulting in cap rate compression at sale and a 16.8% net IRR to investors.

deals.title: Sunbelt Industrial Portfolio
deals.summary: Three-property industrial portfolio across key Sunbelt logistics markets.
deals.full_description: The Sunbelt Industrial Portfolio delivered consistent cash flow throughout the hold period and benefited from cap rate compression at exit, generating a 12.1% net IRR to investors.

deals.title: Urban Mixed-Use Village
deals.summary: High-performing mixed-use development in an infill Seattle neighborhood.
deals.full_description: Urban Mixed-Use Village combined ground-floor retail with Class A apartments above. The project achieved strong lease-up, rent growth, and a favorable sale to an institutional buyer, generating an 18.2% net IRR and 2.1x equity multiple for investors.

deals.title: Gateway Business Park
deals.summary: Closed industrial park investment with strong realized returns in Columbus.
deals.full_description: Gateway Business Park was acquired off-market and benefited from substantial rent growth and tenant expansions, ultimately delivering a 20.5% net IRR and 2.3x equity multiple upon sale in early 2023.
